Title: Phytosulfokine signalling blocks mycotoxin toxicity in Arabidopsis and mediates suppression of cell death activated by bacterial microbe-associated molecular patterns

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase (LRR-RLK); phytosulfokine receptor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf expression strongly induced by FB1 at 48 h; further strongly enhanced by FB1 + flg22
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and indicator of leaf viability/photosynthetic capacity
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fumonisin B1 treatment; co-application of PSK; co-application of flg22; alternariol assays in leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for exogenous PSK and flg22 to block FB1-induced cell death and chlorophyll loss
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (pskr1-2, pskr1-6); peptide co-application assays; chlorophyll quantification; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSKR1 transcript was induced more than fivefold by FB1 in leaves. 2. Exogenous PSK protected wild-type plants from FB1 toxicity, but this protection was lost in pskr1 mutants. 3. flg22 failed to rescue pskr1 mutants from FB1-induced cell death, placing PSK signalling downstream of flg22-mediated protection.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing PSKR1 signalling could improve tolerance to mycotoxin-induced tissue damage and possibly resistance to necrotrophic pathogens; must preserve receptor-complex function with BAK1.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; quantitative proteomics (iTRAQ LC-MS/MS) at whole-seedling level, no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary mechanistic focus of the paper. Although chlorophyll is quantified, the broader process affected is PSK-mediated preservation of photosynthetic/Calvin-cycle capacity and suppression of toxin-triggered cell death.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SERK family leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase; co-receptor kinase
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf transcript suppressed by FB1 at 48 h; suppression prevented in FB1 + flg22 treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and indicator of leaf viability/photosynthetic capacity
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fumonisin B1 treatment; co-application of PSK; co-application of flg22
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential co-receptor for PSK-mediated protection against FB1-induced cell death and chlorophyll degradation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (bak1-3, bak1-4); peptide co-application assays; chlorophyll quantification; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BAK1 transcript was strongly suppressed by FB1. 2. PSK failed to rescue bak1 mutants from FB1 toxicity, demonstrating BAK1 requirement for PSK protection. 3. flg22 prevented FB1-mediated suppression of BAK1 expression, consistent with PSK signalling operating downstream of MAMP perception.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining BAK1-dependent receptor signalling may support broad toxin/stress resilience, but deployment must consider its pleiotropic roles in immunity and development.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic because BAK1 is a shared co-receptor in multiple signalling pathways.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; quantitative proteomics (iTRAQ LC-MS/MS), no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because its role here is receptor co-signalling rather than metabolic catalysis or transcriptional regulation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and indicator of leaf viability/photosynthetic capacity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for endogenous PSK peptide maturation and thereby for basal protection against FB1 toxicity; sbt3.8 mutants are hypersensitive to FB1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (sbt3.8-1, sbt3.8-2); leaf damage assessment; chlorophyll quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sbt3.8 mutants were highly sensitive to a low FB1 dose that caused little damage in wild type. 2. This supports a requirement for PSK precursor processing in protection from toxin-induced cell death. 3. Endogenous mature PSK production contributes to basal FB1 tolerance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering efficient PSK precursor processing may strengthen endogenous peptide-based toxin tolerance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers its metabolic relevance through PSK maturation rather than direct metabolite biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Rubisco large subunit; carboxylase/oxygenase catalytic subunit
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting step of the Calvin cycle in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes carboxylation of ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate using CO2 to generate two molecules of 3-phosphoglycerate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for carbon fixation supplying metabolic building blocks and photosynthetic capacity associated with chlorophyll preservation and survival
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript strongly suppressed by lethal FB1 before visible symptoms; massively upregulated by FB1 + PSK; protein abundance increased in FB1 + PSK relative to FB1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FB1 suppresses RBCL expression, whereas PSK restores/increases Rubisco capacity, supporting Calvin cycle function during toxin challenge
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: iTRAQ quantitative proteomics; qRT-PCR time course under FB1 ± PSK
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Proteomics identified RBCL as increased when PSK was co-applied with FB1. 2. qRT-PCR showed drastic shutdown of RBCL by FB1 and massive induction by PSK co-treatment before cell death onset. 3. The study links PSK-mediated protection to restoration of the Calvin cycle rate-limiting step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining Rubisco expression/activity in toxin-stressed tissues could improve resilience, but RBCL itself is plastid-encoded and may be better targeted through upstream signalling such as PSK.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ LC-MS/MS proteomics; qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although RBCL is not the primary signalling gene of the paper, it is a core experimentally validated metabolic target emphasized in the Results.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the holoenzyme catalyzing the rate-limiting Calvin cycle step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Structural/regulatory small subunit component of Rubisco holoenzyme supporting RuBP carboxylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports catalytic complex required for carbon fixation and maintenance of photosynthetic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript not significantly affected by FB1 alone but massively upregulated in FB1 + PSK; protein abundance increased in FB1 + PSK relative to FB1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PSK enhances RBCS1A expression during FB1 challenge, supporting restoration of Rubisco/Calvin-cycle capacity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: iTRAQ quantitative proteomics; qRT-PCR time course under FB1 ± PSK
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBCS1A protein increased in FB1 + PSK compared with FB1 alone. 2. Transcript was massively induced by FB1 + PSK despite little effect of FB1 alone. 3. PSK promotes the Rubisco holoenzyme gene program under toxin stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used with signalling-based strategies to sustain photosynthesis under toxin stress.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ LC-MS/MS proteomics; qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Small subunit is a structural component rather than catalytic core, but treated here as pathway gene because it is part of the key carbon-fixation enzyme complex highlighted by the study.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the holoenzyme catalyzing the rate-limiting Calvin cycle step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Structural/regulatory small subunit component of Rubisco holoenzyme supporting RuBP carboxylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports catalytic complex required for carbon fixation and maintenance of photosynthetic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript suppressed by FB1 and restored by FB1 + PSK; protein abundance increased in FB1 + PSK relative to FB1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PSK reverses FB1-mediated suppression of RBCS3B, contributing to restored Rubisco/Calvin-cycle function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: iTRAQ quantitative proteomics; qRT-PCR time course under FB1 ± PSK
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FB1 suppressed RBCS3B transcript abundance. 2. PSK restored RBCS3B expression and increased protein abundance relative to FB1 alone. 3. This supports a PSK-driven restoration of carbon fixation machinery during toxin stress.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for sustaining photosynthetic performance under toxin challenge when combined with upstream protective signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: iTRAQ LC-MS/MS proteomics; qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper specifically validated this Rubisco subunit by both proteomics and qRT-PCR.
